title: Fried Katsuramuki Korean Monterey Jack Cheese Kalguksu
description: A crispy, indulgent Korean dish featuring katsuramuki-cut Monterey Jack cheese, served in a kalguksu-inspired style.
instructions: Cut the Monterey Jack cheese into katsuramuki-cut shapes.
Season the cheese cutouts with salt and black pepper.
In a shallow dish, mix together the all-purpose gluten-free flour and cornstarch.
Dip each cheese cutout in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Dip the floured cheese in the beaten egg, making sure it's fully coated.
Roll the egg-coated cheese in the Panko breadcrumbs to coat evenly.
Heat about 1/2 inch of sesame oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the breaded cheese cutouts and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Repeat with the remaining cheese cutouts.
Remove the fried cheese from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve the fried cheese hot, garnished with chopped green onions if desired
